title: Under Pressure From Trump, Nobel Peace Prize Director Defends Award’s Independence
title_cn: 在特朗普施压下，诺贝尔和平奖主任捍卫奖项独立性

paragraphs.en: | Photography by Andrea Gjestvang for WSJ
paragraphs.cn: | 摄影：Andrea Gjestvang 为《华尔街日报》
2026年1月31日 美国东部时间中午12:00

paragraphs.en: OSLO—The director of the Nobel Peace Prize works behind an ornate wooden desk next to a wall adorned with the black-and-white portraits of his seven predecessors. All of them came under political pressure at some point.
paragraphs.cn: 奥斯陆——诺贝尔和平奖主任在一张华丽的木制书桌后工作，旁边是一面墙，墙上装饰着他七位前任的黑白肖像。他们都曾在某个时候承受过政治压力。

paragraphs.en: “What we do is ultimately very simple: We have to award one prize a year,” Kristian Harpviken said in an interview from his office near Oslo’s picturesque bay. “But getting there can be fairly complicated,” he said, acknowledging that such a prize is inherently political.
paragraphs.cn: “我们所做的最终非常简单：我们每年必须颁发一个奖项，”克里斯蒂安·哈普维肯在他位于奥斯陆风景如画的海湾附近的办公室接受采访时说。“但要做到这一点可能相当复杂，”他承认，这样的奖项本质上是政治性的。

paragraphs.en: Such understatement is characteristic of the 64-year-old trained sociologist and part-time farmer who took over leadership of the Norwegian Nobel Institute in January last year, and now works closely with a five-member committee that selects a laureate to be announced each fall.
paragraphs.cn: 这种轻描淡写是这位64岁的受过训练的社会学家兼兼职农民的典型特征，他于去年一月接管了挪威诺贝尔研究所的领导权，现在与一个五人委员会密切合作，该委员会每年秋季选出一位获奖者并公布。

paragraphs.en: In his second year on the job, he has found himself under perhaps greater pressure than anyone who held the role before him.
paragraphs.cn: 在他任职的第二年，他发现自己承受的压力可能比他之前的任何人都大。

paragraphs.en: President Trump has campaigned openly to win the prize, saying he ended more than eight wars in his first year in office. He has expressed frustration that the committee instead awarded it to Venezuela’s María Corina Machado, and this month he connected that disappointment with his bid to take control of Greenland.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普总统曾公开竞选该奖项，称他在上任第一年结束了八场以上的战争。他对委员会反而将奖项授予委内瑞拉的玛丽亚·科丽娜·马查多表示沮丧，本月他将这种失望与他争取控制格陵兰岛的努力联系起来。

paragraphs.en: “Considering your country decided not to give me the Nobel Peace Prize for having stopped 8 Wars PLUS,” Trump wrote in a message to Norwegian Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre, according to Støre, “I no longer feel an obligation to think purely of Peace.”
paragraphs.cn: 据斯托尔说，特朗普在给挪威首相乔纳斯·加尔·斯托尔的信息中写道：“考虑到贵国决定不因我停止了8场战争而授予我诺贝尔和平奖，我不再觉得有义务纯粹考虑和平。”

paragraphs.en: Støre said he explained to Trump that the Norwegian government doesn’t select the laureates. They are chosen by an independent committee appointed by parliament.
paragraphs.cn: 斯托尔表示，他向特朗普解释说，挪威政府不选择获奖者。他们是由议会任命的一个独立委员会选出的。

paragraphs.en: Harpviken declined to comment on Trump’s bid to secure the prize or the controversy surrounding it, but the campaign by the U.S. president has already pushed Harpviken’s institute to speak out publicly more than it is accustomed to doing. When Machado met with Trump and presented him with her Nobel Peace Prize in a meeting earlier this year, the institute in Oslo issued a rare statement saying the prize cannot be transferred.
paragraphs.cn: 哈普维肯拒绝就特朗普争取获得该奖项或围绕该奖项的争议发表评论，但这位美国总统的竞选活动已经促使哈普维肯的研究所比以往更频繁地公开表态。今年早些时候，当马查多与特朗普会面并将她的诺贝尔和平奖颁发给他时，奥斯陆的研究所发表了一份罕见的声明，称该奖项不可转让。

paragraphs.en: Still, it is far from clear that Trump’s public statements are doing much to help his cause. If anything, they may be hurting his chances of winning the prize.
paragraphs.cn: 然而，特朗普的公开声明是否对他的事业有很大帮助，仍远不清楚。如果说有什么影响的话，它们可能正在损害他获奖的机会。

paragraphs.en: “His statement to our prime minister, that now he doesn’t want to take peace into consideration, since he didn’t receive the peace prize, is a very problematic statement for a possible peace prize candidate,” said Berit Reiss-Andersen, a Norwegian lawyer who chaired the committee that awards the prize until 2023.
paragraphs.cn: “他对我们首相的声明，即既然他没有获得和平奖，他就不再考虑和平，对于一个可能的和平奖候选人来说，这是一个非常有问题的主张，”挪威律师贝里特·赖斯-安德森说，她曾担任颁奖委员会主席直至2023年。

paragraphs.en: In a statement to The Wall Street Journal, the White House said Trump didn’t care about the recognition but deserved to win the prize: “His direct involvement in major conflicts, leveraging tools from America’s military might to our superior consumer market, has brought peace to decadeslong wars around the world.”
paragraphs.cn: 白宫在给《华尔街日报》的一份声明中表示，特朗普不关心这一认可，但他理应获奖：“他直接参与了重大冲突，利用从美国军事力量到我们卓越消费市场的一切工具，为世界各地长达数十年的战争带来了和平。”

paragraphs.en: The recent dispute isn’t the first time the institute and Norway have drawn the ire of governments over the awarding of the prize. Controversy has been a staple of the prize’s 125-year history. But the most notable disputes have largely arisen when authoritarian governments protest awards given to dissidents.
paragraphs.cn: 最近的争议并非该研究所和挪威首次因颁奖而招致政府的愤怒。争议一直是该奖项125年历史中的常态。但最引人注目的争议主要发生在威权政府抗议将奖项授予持不同政见者时。

paragraphs.en: “That somebody on their own behalf campaigns for their own candidacy, I cannot think of any example of that,” said Reiss-Andersen. “On a personal level, to say, ‘I want, I want, I want,’ people may feel that, but we are taught that good manners is to suppress such immodest cravings.”
paragraphs.cn: “有人为自己的候选资格而竞选，我无法想到任何这样的例子，”赖斯-安德森说。“就个人而言，说‘我想要，我想要，我想要’，人们可能会有这种感觉，但我们被教导好礼仪是抑制这种不谦虚的渴望。”

paragraphs.en: The Soviet Union vocally protested the prize to dissident Andrei Sakharov in 1975, arguing that it was aimed at fueling antigovernment sentiment. It banned the physicist from leaving the U.S.S.R. to collect the honor in Oslo. His wife, Yelena Bonner, went in his stead.
paragraphs.cn: 苏联曾强烈抗议1975年将奖项授予持不同政见者安德烈·萨哈罗夫，声称此举旨在煽动反政府情绪。它禁止这位物理学家离开苏联前往奥斯陆领奖。他的妻子叶莲娜·邦纳代他前往。

paragraphs.en: After the committee awarded the 2010 prize to imprisoned Chinese dissident Liu Xiaobo, Beijing blasted Norway’s government and curtailed imports of Norwegian salmon. It also placed Liu’s wife, the poet Liu Xia, under de facto house arrest.
paragraphs.cn: 委员会将2010年奖项授予被监禁的中国异议人士刘晓波后，北京抨击挪威政府并限制挪威三文鱼进口。它还将刘晓波的妻子、诗人刘霞软禁。

paragraphs.en: “They absolutely refused to accept the argument that the committee is independent,” Harpviken said. “They kept insisting that this is a Norwegian government decision.”
paragraphs.cn: “他们绝对拒绝接受委员会是独立的这一论点，”哈普维肯说。“他们一直坚持认为这是挪威政府的决定。”

paragraphs.en: Liu died in detention in 2017 after being diagnosed with liver cancer.
paragraphs.cn: 刘晓波于2017年被诊断出肝癌后在拘禁中去世。

paragraphs.en: For Harpviken and his colleagues, the challenge lies in acknowledging the fundamentally political nature of a prize for peace—whose criteria include striving for democracy, disarmament and other increasingly contested notions—while safeguarding their independence to make the right choice.
paragraphs.cn: 对于哈普维肯及其同事来说，挑战在于承认和平奖的根本政治性质——其标准包括争取民主、裁军和其他日益受到争议的观念——同时维护其独立性以做出正确选择。

paragraphs.en: Harpviken said neither he nor anyone on his team has had any direct approaches from anyone in Trump’s circle. A spokesman for Støre didn’t respond to a request for comment.
paragraphs.cn: 哈普维肯说，他或他的团队中没有人直接接触过特朗普圈子里的任何人。斯托尔的发言人没有回应置评请求。

paragraphs.en: “There is no way that a peace prize with relevance is not in some ways political,” said Harpviken, whose office is adjacent to the room where the committee deliberates its choices. “But what is nonnegotiable is our independence from government involvement.”
paragraphs.cn: “一个有相关性的和平奖不可能在某种程度上不具政治性，”哈普维肯说，他的办公室毗邻委员会审议选择的房间。“但不可谈判的是我们独立于政府干预。”

paragraphs.en: On the wall opposite the portraits of Harpviken’s predecessors is a reminder of a very different era in Nobel history. A black-and-white photo from 1897 depicts six grizzled men seated behind a table for the Nobel committee’s first ever meeting. Three of them were serving ministers in the Norwegian government.
paragraphs.cn: 在哈普维肯前任肖像对面的墙上，提醒着诺贝尔历史上一个非常不同的时代。一张1897年的黑白照片描绘了六位饱经风霜的男士坐在桌子后面，参加诺贝尔委员会的首次会议。其中三人是挪威政府的在任部长。

paragraphs.en: Today, there is a strict firewall between the government and the people tasked with selecting the Nobel laureate. Harpviken and two other assistants begin the process in January by assessing a large pool of nominations sent to the institute. They whittle it down to a list that is then discussed with the five standing members of the committee.
paragraphs.cn: 如今，政府与负责选择诺贝尔获奖者的人员之间存在严格的防火墙。哈普维肯和另外两名助手于1月开始工作，评估提交给研究所的大量提名。他们将其筛选成一份名单，然后与委员会的五名常任委员讨论。

paragraphs.en: Committee members are banned from holding any government positions, and are advised to think carefully about whom they meet and what events they attend, to uphold the principle of neutrality.
paragraphs.cn: 委员会成员被禁止担任任何政府职务，并被建议仔细考虑他们会见谁以及参加哪些活动，以维护中立原则。

paragraphs.en: Some receive requests for meetings from foreign embassy officials, PR companies or political activists advocating on behalf of a candidate. Lobbyists push for positive media coverage in the Norwegian press.
paragraphs.cn: 一些人收到外国大使馆官员、公关公司或政治活动家代表候选人提出的会面请求。游说者推动挪威媒体进行积极报道。

paragraphs.en: “We’re very sensitive to being unduly influenced,” said Reiss-Andersen. She said each committee member has to weigh the benefit of meeting a contact with useful information about a candidate against any potential damage to the committee’s reputation.
paragraphs.cn: “我们对受到不当影响非常敏感，”赖斯-安德森说。她说，每位委员会成员都必须权衡会见一位拥有候选人有用信息的联系人所带来的好处，以及对委员会声誉可能造成的任何损害。

paragraphs.en: For Harpviken, who doesn’t get a vote on who is pronounced the winner, safeguarding the prize’s independence is particularly crucial in a world in which alliances are rapidly shifting and belief in notions such as democracy and universal disarmament are being questioned.
paragraphs.cn: 对于没有投票权决定谁是获奖者的哈普维肯来说，在一个联盟迅速变化、对民主和普遍裁军等观念的信仰受到质疑的世界中，维护奖项的独立性尤为关键。

paragraphs.en: “Some people come to me and say, ‘How in the world can you award a Nobel Peace Prize given the world is the way it is?’” he said. “My answer is: It’s never been more important than now.”
paragraphs.cn: “有些人来找我说，‘世界变成这样，你们怎么还能颁发诺贝尔和平奖？’”他说。“我的回答是：现在比以往任何时候都更重要。”

paragraphs.en: Write to Matthew Luxmoore at matthew.luxmoore@wsj.com
paragraphs.cn: 请发送邮件至 Matthew Luxmoore (matthew.luxmoore@wsj.com)

vocabulary.meaning_en: Made in an intricate shape or decorated with complex patterns.
vocabulary.example: The director of the Nobel Peace Prize works behind an ornate wooden desk next to a wall adorned with the black-and-white portraits of his seven predecessors.
vocabulary.example_cn: 诺贝尔和平奖主任在一张华丽的木制书桌后工作，旁边是一面墙，墙上装饰着他七位前任的黑白肖像。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Decorated; made more beautiful or attractive.
vocabulary.example: The director of the Nobel Peace Prize works behind an ornate wooden desk next to a wall adorned with the black-and-white portraits of his seven predecessors.
vocabulary.example_cn: 诺贝尔和平奖主任在一张华丽的木制书桌后工作，旁边是一面墙，墙上装饰着他七位前任的黑白肖像。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A person who held a job or office before the current holder.
vocabulary.example: The director of the Nobel Peace Prize works behind an ornate wooden desk next to a wall adorned with the black-and-white portraits of his seven predecessors.
vocabulary.example_cn: 诺贝尔和平奖主任在一张华丽的木制书桌后工作，旁边是一面墙，墙上装饰着他七位前任的黑白肖像。

vocabulary.meaning_en: The presentation of something as being smaller, worse, or less important than it actually is.
vocabulary.example: Such understatement is characteristic of the 64-year-old trained sociologist and part-time farmer who took over leadership of the Norwegian Nobel Institute in January last year...
vocabulary.example_cn: 这种轻描淡写是这位64岁的受过训练的社会学家兼兼职农民的典型特征，他于去年一月接管了挪威诺贝尔研究所的领导权...

vocabulary.meaning_en: Typical of a particular person, place, or thing.
vocabulary.example: Such understatement is characteristic of the 64-year-old trained sociologist and part-time farmer who took over leadership of the Norwegian Nobel Institute in January last year...
vocabulary.example_cn: 这种轻描淡写是这位64岁的受过训练的社会学家兼兼职农民的典型特征，他于去年一月接管了挪威诺贝尔研究所的领导权...

vocabulary.meaning_en: A person who is honored with an award for outstanding achievement in a particular field.
vocabulary.example: ...now works closely with a five-member committee that selects a laureate to be announced each fall.
vocabulary.example_cn: ...现在与一个五人委员会密切合作，该委员会每年秋季选出一位获奖者并公布。

vocabulary.meaning_en: The feeling of being upset or annoyed as a result of being unable to change or achieve something.
vocabulary.example: He has expressed frustration that the committee instead awarded it to Venezuela’s María Corina Machado, and this month he connected that disappointment with his bid to take control of Greenland.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他对委员会反而将奖项授予委内瑞拉的玛丽亚·科丽娜·马查多表示沮丧，本月他将这种失望与他争取控制格陵兰岛的努力联系起来。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Prolonged public disagreement or heated discussion.
vocabulary.example: Harpviken declined to comment on Trump’s bid to secure the prize or the controversy surrounding it...
vocabulary.example_cn: 哈普维肯拒绝就特朗普争取获得该奖项或围绕该奖项的争议发表评论...

vocabulary.meaning_en: Move from one place to another.
vocabulary.example: When Machado met with Trump and presented him with her Nobel Peace Prize in a meeting earlier this year, the institute in Oslo issued a rare statement saying the prize cannot be transferred.
vocabulary.example_cn: 今年早些时候，当马查多与特朗普会面并将她的诺贝尔和平奖颁发给他时，奥斯陆的研究所发表了一份罕见的声明，称该奖项不可转让。

vocabulary.meaning_en: The fact or condition of being a candidate.
vocabulary.example: “That somebody on their own behalf campaigns for their own candidacy, I cannot think of any example of that,” said Reiss-Andersen.
vocabulary.example_cn: “有人为自己的候选资格而竞选，我无法想到任何这样的例子，”赖斯-安德森说。
